content.claim: Hungary operates one of the most selective and segregated school systems in Europe, with the lowest socio-economic inclusion index among developed countries, demonstrating segregation stronger than Austria, Germany, Lithuania, and the Netherlands.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: CEU working paper is from 2020 and predates current structural changes
Segregation attribution specifically to the gimnázium track structure versus free primary-school choice is contested
Roma segregation is documented as a separate legal-systemic issue requiring distinct policy responses
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
